channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Asia Crypto Rules Boost Exec Personal Liability, D&O Insurance Scrutiny
item.description: Asia's crypto hubs in Hong Kong, Singapore, and South Korea are tightening rules, holding senior managers personally accountable. This means Directors' and Officers' (D&O) liability insurance needs a closer look. Meanwhile, complex scams like 'pig butchering' continue to target investors, showing the ongoing dangers in crypto.

item.title: US Treasury Targets Stablecoins with Strict Rules to Fight Crime
item.description: The U.S. Treasury is proposing new rules for stablecoin issuers that will require firms to block, freeze, and reject illicit transactions. This move aims to strengthen compliance with the Bank Secrecy Act and protect the U.S. financial system against national security threats and criminal activity in the growing stablecoin sector.

item.title: BEML, DMRC Team for West Asia Metro Projects
item.description: Indian public sector firms BEML Limited and Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) are partnering to bid for metro projects in West Asia. The collaboration will offer comprehensive end-to-end solutions, including BEML's rolling stock supplies alongside DMRC's operational expertise. The partnership also plans joint development of indigenous Tunnel Boring Machines (TBMs) and propulsion systems.

item.title: India's Coal Demand Falls Below Forecast as Renewables Surge
item.description: India's coal-fired power plants are projected to consume less than 850 million tonnes of coal in FY27, falling below the 906 million tonne demand communicated by the Power Ministry. This revised forecast accounts for robust renewable energy integration and lower-than-expected demand. Coal stocks remain ample at power plants and mines, ensuring supply security amidst geopolitical concerns.

item.title: SEBI Bars Pledged Shares During IPO Lock-in Periods
item.description: India's market regulator, SEBI, has introduced a new rule preventing pledged shares from being transferred during an IPO's mandatory lock-in period. This move closes a significant regulatory gap, stopping promoters and early investors from bypassing holding rules. Companies must now update their legal documents, inform lenders, and make public disclosures about these shares.

item.title: Info Edge Q4 Billings Up 7.4% Driven by Recruitment; Jeevansathi Soars 20.9%
item.description: Info Edge reported Q4 FY26 billings grew 7.45% to ₹1,057 crore, with full-year billings up 10.3%. Recruitment solutions saw 9.5% growth despite economic uncertainty. Jeevansathi surged 20.9%, while 99acres saw modest gains and Shiksha faced AI-driven traffic dips.

item.title: India Hits 150 GW Renewable Energy Milestone, Solar & Wind Surge
item.description: India's installed renewable energy capacity has surged past 150 GW, driven by a record 44.61 GW of solar installations in fiscal year 2025-26. Wind power also saw its highest-ever installations at 6.05 GW. This expansion cements India's position as the third-largest globally in renewable energy, with total non-fossil capacity reaching 223.27 GW.

item.title: India Sees Oil Price Drop After Ceasefire, But Hormuz Risks Persist
item.description: India's Ministry of External Affairs welcomed the West Asia ceasefire, leading to a significant 14% drop in Brent crude prices to $94 per barrel. This offers immediate relief for India's energy imports and domestic fuel costs. The situation, however, highlights India's deep reliance on the Strait of Hormuz, exposing ongoing supply chain fragilities and strategic vulnerabilities.

item.title: Indian Banks Cut NPAs to 2%, Boost Credit 13.8% Amid Global Uncertainty
item.description: Indian banks achieved a two-year low for gross non-performing assets (NPAs) at 2% by December 2025, down from 2.5% a year earlier. Meanwhile, bank credit grew strongly by 13.8% year-on-year by March 15, 2026, supported by economic activity and supportive monetary policy. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) stated the sector faces no systemic risks despite global pressures including supply chain disruptions.

item.title: India's FMCG Sector Growth Cools as Rural Demand Meets Margin Pressure
item.description: India's FMCG sector value growth moderated to 6% in FY26, a significant deceleration from 9.5% in FY25. Non-urban regions spearheaded this expansion, registering 6.7% growth against urban areas' 4.9%. Staples and dairy led category performance, showing robust double-digit gains. However, the sector grapples with rising raw material costs, exacerbated by geopolitical tensions, and selective consumer spending, indicating a normalization phase rather than broad-based recovery, with discretionary segments facing pressure.

item.title: Indian Steelmakers Lift Prices by Up to ₹3,500/Tonne on Soaring Costs
item.description: Indian steel companies are boosting hot-rolled coil prices by ₹3,500/tonne and cold-rolled coils by ₹3,000/tonne. NMDC Limited also increased iron ore prices by 11%. These hikes follow surges in raw material and energy costs, compounded by supply chain disruptions and rising shipping expenses due to regional conflicts, indicating a focus on protecting profit margins.

item.title: World Bank Lifts India Growth Forecast to 6.6% Amid Global Pressures
item.description: The World Bank raised India's growth forecast for the 2026-27 fiscal year to 6.6%, adding 0.3 percentage points. This figure, however, shows a slowdown from the previous year's 7.6% projection. The revision acknowledges strong domestic demand but points to global economic slowdown, geopolitical tensions, and higher energy prices as key challenges for exports and inflation.

item.title: JM Financial Downgrades Swiggy Instamart, Cites Shareholder Value Risk
item.description: JM Financial downgraded Swiggy's quick commerce unit, Instamart. The brokerage warned that prioritizing short-term profit over market share defense risks destroying shareholder value and making Instamart irrelevant. Despite Swiggy's solid food delivery business, Instamart's losses mean the combined company remains unprofitable. JM Financial suggests an acquisition may be the best path for investors.

item.title: JFE Backs India Waste Projects, But High Debt Sparks Concern
item.description: Japan's JFE Engineering bought a 25% stake for ₹128 crore in Antony Waste Handling Cell's Indian waste-to-energy projects, the first Japanese FDI in the sector. JFE will handle engineering and construction for facilities in Kadapa and Kurnool, costing ₹650-700 crore. While boosting Antony Waste's growth, the projects' heavy debt (70-75%) and fixed ₹8.10/unit PPA rate pose financial risks.

item.title: Pharos Network Lands $44M at $1B Valuation for RWA Blockchain
item.description: Pharos Network, a blockchain focused on tokenizing real-world assets (RWAs), has secured $44 million in Series A funding, achieving a $1 billion valuation. The round, led by traditional finance and crypto investors including Sumitomo Corporation's venture arm and Flow Traders, precedes the mainnet launch of its "asset-native" blockchain. This development highlights growing institutional interest in bringing assets like bonds and private credit onto blockchain infrastructure.

item.title: World Bank: South Asia Growth to 6.3% in 2026 Amid Global Risks
item.description: The World Bank forecasts South Asia's economic growth to slow to 6.3% in 2026, down from an estimated 7% in 2025, due to global energy market disruptions. While India's strong domestic demand and trade deals help the region perform better than others, significant risks remain. These include geopolitical tensions from West Asia, climate shocks, and the effects of AI, all calling for policy changes to ensure stability and create jobs.

item.title: Standard Chartered Aims to Fully Buy Crypto Custodian Zodia
item.description: Standard Chartered PLC is reportedly aiming to fully acquire crypto custody provider Zodia Custody Ltd. The move would merge Zodia's operations into one of Standard Chartered's digital asset divisions, enhancing its institutional crypto services. This potential acquisition highlights increased institutional demand and growth in digital assets as global regulations take shape.

item.title: RBI Gives Bank Chairs Sole Agenda Power After HDFC Scrutiny
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India has released draft rules tightening bank board oversight. Board chairpersons will now have sole responsibility for setting meeting agendas, a key change following the HDFC Bank situation. This move aims to increase accountability for financial performance and governance standards, ensuring critical issues are clearly addressed and directors are better-informed.

item.title: Renaissance Global Cuts Debt by Rs 123 Crore Amid Global Market Concerns
item.description: Renaissance Global slashed Rs 123 crore from its gross debt in Q4 FY26, a 20% drop (24% at constant currency). This strengthens its balance sheet and should cut interest costs. However, the company's 35x P/E valuation and heavy reliance on international markets create a complex picture. Investors should note its B2B focus and currency exposure, especially when compared to higher-valued rivals like Titan.

item.title: RBI to Simplify NBFC Oversight: New 3-Tier System
item.description: India's central bank, the RBI, is introducing a new 3-tier system ('Top', 'Middle', 'Bottom') for non-banking financial companies (NBFCs). This aims to match supervision to each firm's size and risk, and could exempt very small NBFCs from registration. The changes mean updated rules and compliance for the sector.

item.title: GQG Partners Sells ₹197 Crore ITC Hotels Stake
item.description: Investment firm GQG Partners sold 1.3 crore ITC Hotels shares, a 0.62% stake, for ₹197 crore in a block deal on April 8. The sale occurred as ITC Hotels announced a 77% surge in third-quarter net profit to ₹235 crore, driven by significant revenue growth despite one-off expenses.

item.title: White House: Stablecoin Yields Don't Hurt Lending, Study Finds
item.description: A White House report directly disputes banking industry claims that stablecoin yields harm lending. Economists found banning these yields would have negligible impact on credit creation, challenging a core argument used to stall crypto legislation. The study suggests the benefits of competitive consumer returns outweigh potential harms to bank lending.

item.title: West Asia War Fuels BMW India EV Boom: Sales Up 83%, Record Q1
item.description: BMW Group India reported record Q1 car sales, with luxury EVs driving 17% growth. Rising fuel price concerns, linked to the West Asia war, are significantly boosting demand, with EV sales up 83%. The company is optimizing supply to meet a 1-2 month waiting period for its electric vehicles.

item.title: India Turns Industrial Waste into Strategic Metal Powders
item.description: India's metal powder sector is becoming strategically vital, driven by demand in automotive, engineering, and electronics. Innovations in transforming industrial waste into usable powders are gaining traction, aiming to reduce import dependence and enhance domestic manufacturing capabilities. Technological advancements are key to ensuring material consistency and scalability for high-precision applications.

item.title: India's Women Drive ₹76 Lakh Crore Credit Boom, Sparking Entrepreneurship
item.description: Women are spearheading India's credit expansion, holding a ₹76 lakh crore portfolio and representing 26% of total lending. This growth marks a significant shift from microfinance to retail and business loans, boosted by India's digital infrastructure.

item.title: RBI Proposes Dropping Due Diligence for MSMEs on TReDS Platforms
item.description: India's central bank, the RBI, is proposing to remove the mandatory due diligence for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) when they join Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS) platforms. This aims to simplify business processes and help more MSMEs get faster working capital. The RBI plans to issue draft directions for public feedback.

item.title: India's Digital Rupee Hits 150M+ Transactions, RBI Eyes Future
item.description: India's central bank digital currency (CBDC) has seen robust adoption, with transactions surpassing 150 million and a total value exceeding Rs 34,000 crore. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is actively developing key features like programmability, viewing CBDC as the future of payments. However, the central bank is adopting a gradual rollout strategy, emphasizing readiness and cross-border integration over a swift launch.

item.title: RITES Gains ₹39 Crore Boost on NALCO Railway Contract
item.description: RITES Limited has secured an expanded contract from NALCO for its Utkal-D coal mines railway project. The deal, covering detailed engineering and project management, has been revised upwards by ₹39 crore, bringing the total value to ₹118.89 crore (excluding GST). This amendment strengthens RITES' order book.

item.title: Bernstein: Bitcoin's Quantum Threat Manageable, Upgrade Cycle Ahead
item.description: Wall Street broker Bernstein assesses the quantum computing threat to Bitcoin as credible but manageable. While recent advances compress timelines for potential attacks on cryptography, scaling quantum systems remains a complex challenge. Bernstein views this as a medium-to-long-term upgrade cycle, not an existential risk, with industry experts anticipating a 3-5 year transition to post-quantum cryptography.

item.title: India Railways Targets 3,000MT Freight Capacity, Market Share Lagging
item.description: Indian Railways aims to boost freight capacity to 3,000 million tonnes by 2030. However, it faces challenges including a lagging market share, capacity limits, wagon shortages, poor last-mile links, and regulatory barriers for private investment, which keep logistics costs high.

item.title: HPCL Rajasthan Refinery Cost Hits ₹73,000 Cr; CCEA OKs Hike
item.description: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) sanctioned a significant cost increase for the HPCL Rajasthan Refinery Limited (HRRL) project, pushing the total expenditure to approximately ₹73,000 crore. This represents a nearly 69% jump from the original ₹43,129 crore estimate. Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) will infuse an additional ₹9,000 crore in equity. The project, crucial for petrochemical production and energy independence, now targets commercial operation by July 2026.

item.title: Tech Mahindra Stock Gets Analyst Buy Call, ₹1,620 Target
item.description: Brokerage analysts have initiated a 'Buy' rating on Tech Mahindra, setting a ₹1,620 price target and signaling a potential 10% upside. The positive call is fueled by record Q3FY26 bookings of $1.1 billion, significant deal wins, and reaffirmed FY27 goals, including a 15% EBIT margin, driven by AI modernization opportunities.

item.title: Google Search Glitch Halts India Market Report Creation
item.description: Repeated failures to find accurate economic and market data through Google Search have halted the creation of a key market report. Irrelevant search results prevented the collection of essential information for competitor analysis, historical context, and understanding analyst sentiment, making it impossible to produce a high-impact, data-driven report.

item.title: Dollar Index Plunges Over 1% Amid Mideast Unrest, Trade Fears
item.description: The US Dollar Index (DXY) plummeted over 1% on Wednesday, nearing 98.58, driven by renewed Middle East conflict and concerns over potential Strait of Hormuz transit fees. This sharp decline threatens to erase the greenback's gains for 2026. While a fragile ceasefire initially offered relief, subsequent attacks have reignited geopolitical uncertainty. The euro, yen, and pound strengthened against the dollar, underscoring broader currency market dynamics beyond regional tensions. The DXY's trajectory now hinges on de-escalation and the economic fallout from potential trade disruptions.

item.title: India's Food Safety Push: States Drive Inspections, Fines Top ₹154 Crore
item.description: India has sharply boosted food safety enforcement in FY 2025-26, with states leading the charge. Over 165,000 food samples were analyzed, showing a 17.16% failure rate, resulting in ₹154.87 crore in penalties and 945 product recalls. More than 1 million street food vendors are now under formal oversight to improve hygiene and consumer protection nationwide.

item.title: PPFAS Asset Management Enters Pension Fund Market After PFRDA Approval
item.description: PPFAS Asset Management has received approval from the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) to become a sponsor for a pension fund under the National Pension System (NPS). This clearance allows the firm to enter the retirement savings market by establishing a dedicated pension fund entity to manage these assets.

item.title: KEC International Wins ₹2,518 Crore in New Orders; Stock Jumps
item.description: KEC International has secured new orders valued at ₹2,518 crore spanning its civil, transportation, transmission & distribution, and cables divisions. Key wins include its largest commercial real estate order to date and a joint venture for India’s Kavach train safety system. The company's stock saw a nearly 7% increase following the news.

item.title: JPMorgan: MicroStrategy Buys Fuel Thin Crypto Inflows Amid 70% Q1 Drop
item.description: JPMorgan analysts report a sharp 70% drop in digital asset capital inflows for Q1 2026, falling to $11 billion. The report highlights MicroStrategy's persistent bitcoin purchases and concentrated crypto venture capital funding as the primary drivers sustaining the market. This comes as the overall crypto market capitalization shed nearly 20% during the quarter, with Bitcoin and Ether prices experiencing significant declines before a late-quarter stabilization.

item.title: SEZs Blast Govt DTA Tax Relief as 'Too Narrow'
item.description: Special Economic Zone (SEZ) operators are pushing back against the government's recent tax concessions for domestic tariff area (DTA) sales. Industry representatives argue the policy's scope is too restrictive, with data suggesting up to 80% of supplies may see little to no benefit. SEZ units are demanding higher sales caps and a longer concession period to make the relief meaningful amid weak global demand.

item.title: Coal India Misses FY26 Target as Renewables, Mines Gain Share
item.description: Coal India (CIL) concluded fiscal year 2026 with a production shortfall, delivering 768 million tonnes (MT) against targets and seeing a 1.6% year-on-year volume decline. Extended monsoons, a significant surge in renewable energy's share to 26% of generation, and increased captive mining output eroded CIL's market share to approximately 73%. Despite these headwinds, CIL aims for 815 MT in FY27, banking on projected power demand growth and strategies to boost e-auction sales, though high inventories and an impending wage revision present near-term margin pressures.
